description: During Super Bowl XLIV in 2010, the organization Focus on the Family aired a controversial ad featuring Tim Tebow and his mother, which sparked a national debate over religious advocacy in high-profile marketing. The ad triggered a binary reaction between those supporting the inclusion of such messages in entertainment media and those fiercely opposing the mixing of religion and professional sports marketing.

key_events.description: Announcement of the ad's purchase causes initial media backlash from NOW and other advocacy groups.
Super Bowl XLIV airs; CBS receives record-breaking complaints and praise simultaneously.
National news outlets publish op-eds debating the 'commercialization of faith' on television.
Follow-up marketing research indicates a deep cultural divide in brand sentiment for future religious ads.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring how polarized audiences react to non-traditional advertising content during peak secular cultural events.
